facts.text: Cambridge’s tree‑lined streets hide a Victorian‑era Opera House called Hubbard Hall, one of the village’s few surviving 19th‑century buildings.
The Buskirk Covered Bridge, one of only four historic covered bridges in Washington County, spans the local creek and dates back to the 1800s.
Lake Lauderdale campground offers family‑friendly camping and a scenic lake view, making it a popular outdoor spot for locals and visitors alike.
Every Saturday, a farmers’ market and co‑op grocer bring fresh produce, artisanal goods, and a sense of community to the village center.
Cambridge’s eclectic artist community thrives among family‑owned shops, galleries, and studios, creating a vibrant cultural scene in the heart of the village.
Founded in 1700s at a crossroads, Cambridge grew around historic farms and remains a quiet, picturesque village in Washington County.
